title: Ecosystem Concept Map
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive vis-network concept map showing 20 foundational ecology concepts as nodes with labeled relationship edges, supporting analysis of conceptual interconnections.

learningObjectives: Organize foundational ecology concepts into a network showing their relationships
Explain how ecological concepts connect to and depend on each other
Trace pathways through the concept network to explain ecological processes
Analyze which concepts serve as central hubs connecting multiple ideas
prerequisites: Basic understanding of photosynthesis and cellular respiration

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive concept network exploration with click-to-highlight
keyQuestions: Which concept has the most connections to other concepts and why?
How does Energy connect to Ecosystem through intermediate concepts?
How are Habitat and Niche related but different?
If you removed Water Properties, which concepts would be most affected?
Why is a concept map more useful than a list for understanding ecology?
commonMisconceptions: Ecology concepts are independent topics rather than an interconnected web
Habitat and niche mean the same thing
Abiotic factors are less important than biotic factors in ecosystems
assessmentOpportunities: Pathway tracing exercise connecting distant concepts through intermediate nodes
Identification of the most connected hub concepts with justification
Proposal of new concepts to add to the map with connection descriptions
